entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Dry-season sorghum cultivars evaluated for photoperiod sensitivity and yield in Southern Chad

entities.name: SOWING DATE
entities.description: The date of planting, categorized as early or late, affecting plant traits

entities.name: POTENTIAL YIELD
entities.description: The maximum yield achievable by the sorghum cultivars

entities.name: FLOWERING TIME
entities.description: The time taken for the sorghum plant to reach the flowering stage

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD SENSITIVITY
entities.description: The degree to which the plant's development is influenced by day length

entities.name: SOUTHERN CHAD
entities.description: The region in Chad where the sorghum ecotypes were collected and studied

entities.name: YOUÉ, CHAD
entities.description: The location of the Agricultural Research Extension Farm where trials were conducted

entities.name: ALPHA-LATTICE DESIGN
entities.description: The experimental design used to replicate the trials

entities.name: DURATION OF VEGETATIVE PHASE
entities.description: The length of the plant's growth period before flowering

entities.name: STAYGREEN
entities.description: The trait of maintaining green leaf area during the ripening stage

entities.name: CUMULATIVE GROWING DEGREE UNIT
entities.description: A measure of heat accumulation used to determine flowering requirements

entities.name: DAYLENGTH
entities.description: The duration of light per day, which influences flowering time

entities.name: NIGHT TEMPERATURES
entities.description: The temperature during the night period

entities.name: RESIDUAL MOISTURE
entities.description: Soil moisture remaining from previous seasons used for dry-season cultivation

entities.name: WEST AND CENTRAL AFRICA
entities.description: The broader geographical area where dry-season sorghum is grown

entities.name: YEAR
entities.description: The specific years 2013 and 2014 during which the trials were conducted

relationships.description: Affects the growth and yield of dry-season sorghum
Delaying sowing significantly decreases potential yield
Delaying sowing decreases the duration of the vegetative phase
Used to determine the photoperiod sensitivity of cultivars
Decrease in daylength affects flowering time
Does not significantly affect flowering time
Late sowing requires lower cumulative growing degree units to flower
Cultivars were collected and evaluated in this region
Trials were conducted at the research farm in this location
Experimental design used to evaluate sorghum ecotypes
Year significantly affected the assessed traits including yield
Sowing date did not significantly decrease staygreen
Dry-season sorghum is cultivated on residual moisture
Cultivation occurs in this broader region
